businessSummary.oneLiner: Southeast Asia's largest internet platform — Shopee (e-commerce), SeaMoney (fintech), Garena (gaming) trifecta
businessSummary.description: Sea Limited is a consumer internet company operating across Southeast Asia, Latin America, and globally through Shopee (e-commerce), SeaMoney (digital financial services), and Garena (gaming). FY2025 revenue $22.94B (+36.4% YoY), net income $1.58B (+255%). Thursday close $85.16 (-3.03%). Fell with the broader market selloff (S&P -1.52%) and amplified by e-commerce growth stock pressure. YTD -32.2% from $131.53. Seeking Alpha: 'Too cheap to ignore.' Market cap $48.5B, P/E ~30.7x, Forward P/E ~18x.
howTheyMakeMoney: E-commerce (Shopee): #1 SEA marketplace — GMV fees, ads, logistics (~64% revenue)
Fintech (SeaMoney): SPayLater, SeaBank — lending, payments, insurance (~21% revenue)
Gaming (Garena): Free Fire — global battle royale, in-game purchases (~15% revenue)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on $1.58B net income. Reasonable vs growth peers

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: E-commerce investment expansion pressuring margins. Fintech high-margin

keyMetrics.name: YTD Return
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 EPS miss + e-commerce investment pivot drove selloff

growthDrivers.title: Seeking Alpha: 'Sea Limited — Too Cheap to Ignore'
growthDrivers.description: 3/12: Revenue +36.4%, net income +255%, yet YTD -32%. Forward P/E ~18x at historically lowest level. Management chose e-commerce growth investment for 2026 — market overreaction.

growthDrivers.title: SeaMoney Fintech Boom — 'Southeast Asia's Nubank' Emerges
growthDrivers.description: GL Insight: SeaMoney driving high-margin growth through SPayLater lending expansion and SeaBank deposit growth.

growthDrivers.title: Simply Wall St: 'Still Attractive After 32% YTD Drop'
growthDrivers.description: 3/10: Down 17.8% in 30 days and 32.2% YTD, yet 3-year return +16.3%. Valuation appeal resurfaces.

growthDrivers.title: 11/11 Analysts Strong Buy — Target $179.54 (+111% Upside)
growthDrivers.description: Unanimous Strong Buy consensus with average target $179.54. Current price below even the lowest target ($120).

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Southeast Asia's largest e-commerce (Shopee) + fintech (SeaMoney) + gaming (Garena) triangular ecosystem. 'Amazon of the East' positioning.

moats.description: Shopee leads Southeast Asia by GMV. Self-built logistics network.
700M SEA population + LATAM expansion. Young digital-native markets.

competitors.name: Temu/PDD
competitors.detail: Ultra-low-price e-commerce offensive. Expanding into SEA

recentNews.significance: Fell more than S&P -1.52%. Oil surge linked to e-commerce logistics cost concerns.

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha: 'Sea Limited — Too Cheap to Ignore'
recentNews.significance: Forward P/E ~18x with +36.4% growth. Q4 EPS miss was one-time tax/FX. Market overreaction.

recentNews.headline: Simply Wall St: 'Still attractive after 32% YTD drop?'
recentNews.significance: 30-day -17.8%, YTD -32.2% decline, yet long-term valuation appeal resurfaces. 3-year +16.3%.

recentNews.headline: GL Insight: 'SeaMoney fintech boom and the plummet of Sea Group'
recentNews.significance: SeaMoney high-growth not reflected in stock price. E-commerce investment decision received negatively.

recentNews.headline: Q4 earnings miss triggers -16.5% crash — EPS miss + e-commerce investment pivot
recentNews.significance: Q4 EPS miss from non-operational tax/FX volatility. 2026 e-commerce margin trade-off for growth disappointed market.

analystOpinions.notableComment: All 11 analysts Strong Buy. Average target $179.54 (+111%). Current price below even the lowest target ($120). Extreme disconnect between analyst consensus and market pricing.

risks.title: E-commerce Margin vs Growth Dilemma — 2026 Investment Expansion
risks.description: Management chose e-commerce growth investment over margin optimization. Near-term profitability could deteriorate.

risks.title: YTD -32.2% — Downward Momentum Persists
risks.description: From $131.53 to $85.16. One-month -17.8%. Failed trend reversal could drive further decline.

risks.title: Oil Surge → Logistics Cost Increase + Consumer Weakness
risks.description: SEA e-commerce logistics costs sensitive to oil. Prolonged Iran conflict would further pressure margins.

risks.title: Temu/PDD Ultra-Low-Price Offensive
risks.description: Chinese ultra-low-price e-commerce expanding into Southeast Asia. Could slow Shopee GMV growth.

risks.title: Free Fire Revenue Stagnation
risks.description: Garena gaming revenue growth slowing. E-commerce/fintech offset but gaming profitability contribution declining.

buyReasons.title: Forward P/E ~18x with +36.4% Growth — Historical Floor Valuation
buyReasons.rationale: Revenue $22.94B (+36.4%), net income +255%. PEG ~0.5. Extreme undervaluation vs growth.

buyReasons.title: 11/11 Strong Buy — Target $179.54 (+111%)
buyReasons.rationale: Unanimous analyst buy. Current price below even the lowest target ($120).

buyReasons.title: 'Too Cheap to Ignore' — Contrarian Play on Market Overreaction
buyReasons.rationale: Q4 EPS miss was one-time (tax/FX). E-commerce investment is long-term value creation strategy.

buyReasons.title: SeaMoney Fintech Explosive Growth — Not Reflected in Price
buyReasons.rationale: SPayLater/SeaBank high-growth will long-term offset e-commerce investment costs.

overallOpinion: Sea Limited closed Thursday at $85.16 (-3.03%), extending its YTD decline to -32.2%. The oil surge added e-commerce logistics cost concerns on top of the broader market selloff.

Yet Seeking Alpha argues it's 'too cheap to ignore.' Revenue +36.4%, net income +255%, Forward P/E ~18x. All 11 analysts maintain Strong Buy with average target $179.54 (+111%). The stock trades below even the lowest analyst target of $120.

RSI at 28.5 signals oversold conditions. Technical bounce possible, but with the stock far below both 50-day ($99.50) and 200-day ($108.70) SMAs, a V-shaped recovery may be difficult. Whether management's e-commerce investment decision was right will be revealed in H2 2026.
